description: A small damselfly similar to *Azuragrion nigridorsum* but restricted to the Horn of Africa and southern Arabian Peninsula.
distribution: *Azuragrion somalicum* is found in Somalia and adjacent parts of Ethiopia and Djibouti. It has been observed on the Arabian Peninsula in Yemen and Oman.
sources: Atlas of the Dragonflies and Damselflies of West and Central Asia, J.-P. Boudot, S. Borisov, G. De Knijf, R.H.A. van Grunsven, A. Schröter, V. J. Kalkman
Field guide to the dragonflies of Britain and Europe, Klaas-Douwe B Dijkstra.
